steps.title: Identify the possessive needs
steps.content: The trajectory belongs to the object (singular), and the modeling is done by the astronomers (plural). Both relationships must be shown with possessive forms.

steps.title: Apply the possessive rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: possessive apostrophe placement. Rule: add apostrophe + s for singular nouns; add only an apostrophe after the s for regular plural nouns.
  • Choice A — "astronomers modeling of the objects": neither "astronomers" nor "objects" has an apostrophe; both missing possessives. ✗
  • Choice B — "astronomer's modeling of the object's": "astronomer's" is singular possessive but the subject is plural astronomers. ✗
  • Choice C — "astronomers modeling of the object's": "astronomers" lacks an apostrophe, so the plural possessive is missing. ✗
  • Choice D — "astronomers' modeling of the object's": "astronomers'" is correct plural possessive; "object's" is correct singular possessive. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
